title: First Contact
communicativeGoal: Introduce yourself, greet people, and exchange basic information

chunks.phrase: よろしくお願いします

pragmaticFormulas.phrase: よろしくお願いします

coIntroductionRules.reason: Self-introduction formulas always taught together as a pair
Topic marker and copula form the basic sentence pattern XはYです

title: What Is This?
communicativeGoal: Identify and ask about objects around you

coIntroductionRules.reason: Possessive and question markers enable asking 'Whose is this?' pattern

title: Numbers & Counting
communicativeGoal: Count things and talk about quantities using counters

coIntroductionRules.reason: Numbers 1-3 taught together as initial counting set

title: Where Things Are
communicativeGoal: Describe where things are located and talk about existence

chunks.phrase: 机の上に本があります

coIntroductionRules.reason: Existence verbs for inanimate and animate objects taught as a contrasting pair
上 and 下 taught as spatial antonym pair

title: My Daily Routine
communicativeGoal: Talk about daily activities and tell time

coIntroductionRules.reason: 起きる and 寝る taught as antonym pair for daily routine bookends
Time-of-day greetings taught together

title: Going Places
communicativeGoal: Discuss transportation and describe where you are going

coIntroductionRules.reason: Core movement verbs 行く/来る/帰る taught as a triad
Departure and return greetings taught as a pair

title: Describing Things
communicativeGoal: Describe people, places, and things using adjectives

coIntroductionRules.reason: 大きい/小さい taught as antonym pair
新しい/古い taught as antonym pair
Both adjective types introduced together to learn the distinction early

title: What I Like
communicativeGoal: Express preferences, likes, and dislikes

coIntroductionRules.reason: 好き/嫌い taught as preference antonym pair
上手/下手 taught as ability antonym pair

title: Eating & Drinking
communicativeGoal: Order food, talk about meals, and express hunger

coIntroductionRules.reason: Meal-time formulas いただきます/ごちそうさまでした always taught as a pair
Object marker を leads naturally into をください request pattern

title: Shopping
communicativeGoal: Buy things, ask about prices, and handle money

coIntroductionRules.reason: 高い/安い price adjectives taught together in shopping context

title: Talking About the Past
communicativeGoal: Narrate past events and describe how things were

coIntroductionRules.reason: Temporal trio 昨日/今日/明日 taught together
Year temporal trio 去年/今年/来年 taught together

title: Wanting & Planning
communicativeGoal: Express desires and describe future plans

coIntroductionRules.reason: Want to do (たい) vs want a thing (ほしい) contrasted together

title: Asking Permission
communicativeGoal: Make polite requests and ask for permission

chunks.phrase: 写真を撮ってもいいですか

coIntroductionRules.reason: Permission and prohibition て-form patterns taught as contrasting pair
Positive and negative request patterns taught together

title: Giving Reasons
communicativeGoal: Explain why and give reasons for actions

chunks.phrase: 疲れたので早く寝たい

coIntroductionRules.reason: から (casual) and ので (polite) taught together as register pair for giving reasons

title: Comparing Things
communicativeGoal: Compare items and express superlatives

chunks.phrase: 夏より冬のほうが好き
バスより電車のほうが速い
果物の中でりんごが一番好き
どちらのほうが好きですか

coIntroductionRules.reason: Comparative and superlative patterns taught together

title: Making Plans Together
communicativeGoal: Invite people and suggest activities together

pragmaticFormulas.phrase: ありがとうございます

coIntroductionRules.reason: Invitation (ませんか) and suggestion (ましょう) taught as a functional pair

title: How Things Are Done
communicativeGoal: Describe how actions are performed using adverbs

chunks.phrase: 日曜日にいつも寝ます

coIntroductionRules.reason: Frequency adverb and simultaneous action pattern together show manner of doing things

title: Ability & Possibility
communicativeGoal: Discuss what you can and cannot do

chunks.phrase: 日本語を話すことができる
漢字を読むことができる
車を運転することができない

coIntroductionRules.reason: ことができる and potential form are two ways to express ability, taught together for contrast

title: Giving & Receiving
communicativeGoal: Talk about giving and receiving things and favors

pragmaticFormulas.phrase: ありがとうございます

coIntroductionRules.reason: Giving/receiving triad あげる/もらう/くれる must be taught together to understand perspective differences

title: Sequencing Events
communicativeGoal: Sequence events in time using before, after, and when

coIntroductionRules.reason: 前に and 後で taught as temporal antonym pair

title: At the Doctor
communicativeGoal: Describe symptoms and talk about health

coIntroductionRules.reason: Body part vocabulary 頭/お腹/体 taught together in health context

title: Weather Talk
communicativeGoal: Discuss weather conditions and forecasts

chunks.phrase: 明日は雨だと思います

coIntroductionRules.reason: でしょう (polite) and だろう (casual) taught as register pair for probability
暑い/寒い temperature antonym pair
暖かい/涼しい mild temperature antonym pair

title: Getting Around
communicativeGoal: Give and follow directions to navigate a city

pragmaticFormulas.phrase: ありがとうございます

coIntroductionRules.reason: Direction words 右/左/真っ直ぐ taught together
曲がる and 渡る are key direction-giving verbs

title: Phone & Messages
communicativeGoal: Make phone calls, send messages, and report what others said

chunks.phrase: もう一度言ってください

pragmaticFormulas.phrase: もう一度お願いします

coIntroductionRules.reason: Quotation (と言う) and opinion (と思う) patterns taught together

title: Rules & Obligations
communicativeGoal: Express rules, obligations, and prohibitions

grammar.name: なくてもいい (don't have to)

chunks.phrase: ここに触らないでください
心配しないでください

coIntroductionRules.reason: Must do vs don't have to do contrasted together
Without doing vs have to do — negative form patterns

title: Connecting Ideas
communicativeGoal: Connect ideas fluently using conjunctions and て-form chaining

coIntroductionRules.reason: Contrastive けど and additive し taught together for discourse connectors

title: Expressing Feelings
communicativeGoal: Express emotions and react to situations naturally

pragmaticFormulas.phrase: ありがとうございます

coIntroductionRules.reason: 嬉しい/悲しい emotion antonym pair
怒る/笑う taught as contrasting emotional reactions

title: Review & Integration
communicativeGoal: Use all A1 patterns in mixed real-world contexts

pragmaticFormulas.phrase: よろしくお願いします
ありがとうございます
